@documentation.@comment: One row of the table mapping an allocation message to its fragmentation-related fields.

@properties.msg_type: MsgType(35) wire value identifying the message.
@properties.total_count_field: Field carrying the total number of repeating-group entries across all fragments (e.g. TotNoAllocs).
@properties.fragment_count_field: Field carrying the number of entries in the current message fragment (e.g. NoAllocs).
@properties.principal_message_reference: Principal message reference field used to bind allocation message fragments together (e.g. AllocID, AllocReportID).

@documentation.@comment: One row of the AllocStatus(87) value/description table.

@properties.status_code: Wire status code as referenced in the chapter.
@properties.status_label: Human-readable label of the status code.

@documentation.@comment: One row of a "Key Fields for <area> Application Message References" table identifying the field whose value is copied into BusinessRejectRefID(379).

@properties.category_label: Category label as printed in the source table (free text; may include parenthesised sub-categories such as "Single General Order Handling").
@properties.fix_message: FIX message name with MsgType in parentheses, e.g. AllocationInstruction(35=J).
@properties.business_reject_ref_id_value: Source field copied into BusinessRejectRefID(379) when the target message lacks its own reject. May enumerate several alternatives joined by "or".

@documentation.@comment: A top-level business area of the FIX Latest specification.

@properties.title: Display title of the area.
@properties.categories: Message categories defined within a business area.

@documentation.@comment: One entry of the BusinessRejectReason(380) code table.

@properties.reject_reason_label: Human-readable label of a BusinessRejectReason(380) code.

@documentation.@comment: One message-format row from the Post-Trade Processing Clearing Services section, with its supported actions.

@properties.message_format: Clearing-service message format family referenced in the chapter.
@properties.supported_actions: Action labels (e.g. Allocation, Accept, Reject, Release, Change, Delete) supported by a clearing-service message format.

@documentation.@comment: A component used only by messages within a single business area.

@properties.is_repeating_group: True when the component is a repeating group.

@documentation.@comment: A unit of change to FIX Latest.

@properties.number: Sequential identifier of the Extension Pack.
@properties.title: Short descriptive title.
@properties.enhancement_summary: Narrative summary of what the EP introduces.
@properties.applies_to_session_layer_only: True when the EP applies only to the FIX Session Layer.
@properties.applies_to_fixml_only: True when the EP applies only to the FIXML encoding.

@documentation.@comment: A FIX field — a uniquely tagged data element with a FIX datatype.

@properties.requirement: Required-status of the field within the enclosing context.
@properties.is_user_defined: True when the field is a User Defined Field.

@documentation.@comment: A FIX Protocol datatype.

@properties.value_space: ISO/IEC 11404:2007 GPD value space assigned to the datatype.
@properties.deprecated_for_new_designs: True for datatypes not permitted in new designs.
@properties.external_code_set: Reference standard for datatypes backed by an external code set.
@properties.radix: Numeric radix for scaled value-space datatypes.
@properties.index_lower_bound: Inclusive lower bound of a bounded-array index.
@properties.index_upper_bound: Inclusive upper bound of a bounded-array index.
@properties.minimum_value: Inclusive lower bound on the integer value space.
@properties.maximum_value: Inclusive upper bound on the integer value space.
@properties.footnote_numbers: Footnote indicators attached to a datatype row.

@documentation.@comment: A member standard of the FIX Family of Standards.

@properties.id: Unique identifier (CURIE or local name) of the element.
@properties.name: Display name of the element.
@properties.acronym: Short acronym used to refer to the standard.
@properties.layer: The layer the standard belongs to.
@properties.version: Version of the standard, if applicable.
@properties.session_profile: Name of the session profile for session-layer variants.
@properties.encoding_name: Named encoding, when layer is ENCODING.

@properties.product_committees: Global Product Committees maintained by FPL.

@properties.infra_global_component_repetition: Repetition indicator for the Global Component as it appears in the referenced Infrastructure messages.

@documentation.@comment: One row of the layout table published in the Infrastructure Appendix for a message or component. Each row identifies either a FIX field (by tag number + name) or an embedded component (by name), records whether it is required, carries the Description-column text, and flags whether it appears as a nested child of a repeating-group counter.

@properties.infra_layout_kind: Row kind — either a FIX field (numeric Tag) or an embedded component (literal "Component" in the Tag column of the source table).
@properties.infra_layout_field_tag: FIX tag number drawn from the Tag column when the row is a field (i.e. layout_kind = FIELD). Absent for COMPONENT rows.
@properties.infra_layout_element_name: Element name as printed in the Name column — either the FIX field name (e.g. ApplReqID) or the component name (e.g. StandardHeader, ApplIDRequestGrp).
@properties.infra_layout_required: Whether the field or component is required, as printed in the Req’d column of the source layout table ("Y" / "N").
@properties.infra_layout_description: Free-text content of the Description column of the row (may be empty).
@properties.infra_layout_nested: Whether the row appears nested under a repeating-group counter (i.e. its Tag column is prefixed with the "→" arrow in the source table).

@properties.trade_fragmentation_message: Message that may be fragmented — verbatim text from the Message column of the fragmentation table (e.g. "NewOrderList(35=E)").
@properties.trade_fragmentation_total_entries_field: Name and tag of the "Total Entries" field used to indicate the total count across all fragments of the batch (e.g. "TotNoOrders(68)").
@properties.trade_fragmentation_repeating_group: Verbatim description of the repeating group that may be fragmented — from the table's third column.

@documentation.@comment: One row of the layout table published in the Trade Appendix for a message or component. Each row identifies either a FIX field (by tag number + name) or an embedded component (by name), records whether it is required, carries the Description-column text, and flags whether it appears as a nested child of a repeating-group counter.

@properties.trade_layout_kind: Row kind — either a FIX field (numeric Tag) or an embedded component (literal "Component" in the Tag column of the source table).
@properties.trade_layout_field_tag: FIX tag number drawn from the Tag column when the row is a field (i.e. layout_kind = FIELD). Absent for COMPONENT rows.
@properties.trade_layout_element_name: Element name as printed in the Name column — either the FIX field name or the component name.
@properties.trade_layout_required: Whether the field or component is required, as printed in the Req'd column of the source layout table ("Y" / "N").
@properties.trade_layout_description: Free-text content of the Description column of the row (may be empty).
@properties.trade_layout_nested: Whether the row appears nested under a repeating-group counter (i.e. its Tag column is prefixed with the "→" arrow in the source table).

@documentation.@comment: Per-category message description.

@properties.msg_type: MsgType(35) wire value identifying the message.
@properties.description: Description of the message's purpose and usage.
@properties.trade_layout_url: URL of the detailed message- or component-layout in the Trade Appendix.
@properties.trade_layout_rows: Ordered rows of the layout table published for the message or component in the Trade Appendix.

@documentation.@comment: One row of the area-wide trade messages table.

@properties.msg_type: MsgType(35) wire value identifying the message.

@documentation.@comment: Purpose-grouped sub-section inside a category's Messages sub-section (e.g. "New Order Single", "Execution Reports", "Order Cancel Requests" under Single/General Order Handling).

@properties.trade_group_title: Purpose-group heading inside a category's Messages sub-section.
@properties.description: Description of the purpose-group's role within the category.
@properties.messages: Messages bundled under the purpose-group heading.
@properties.trade_ord_status_precedence_entries: Rows of the OrdStatus(39) precedence table that appears inside the Execution Reports purpose-group of the Single/General Order Handling category.

@documentation.@comment: One row of the OrdStatus(39) precedence table published in the Execution Reports purpose-group of the Single/General Order Handling category. Defines the precedence ranking used to resolve simultaneous state transitions on an order, paired with the human-readable OrdStatus label and its descriptive prose.

@properties.trade_ord_status_precedence: Precedence rank (1 = lowest, higher numbers take precedence) of an OrdStatus(39) value used to resolve simultaneous state transitions on an order.
@properties.trade_ord_status_label: Human-readable OrdStatus(39) label as printed in the Execution Reports precedence table (e.g. "Pending Cancel", "Done for Day", "Filled").
@properties.description: Verbatim OrdStatus description from the table.

@documentation.@comment: A reserved range of tag numbers for User Defined Fields.

@properties.low_tag: Inclusive lower bound of the range.
@properties.high_tag: Upper bound of the tag range. Required for all ``usage`` values except ``GTC_RESERVED`` (which is open-ended, 50000+). Downstream validators should enforce this; the constraint cannot be expressed here because LinkML's ``equals_string`` operator only accepts string-ranged slots and ``usage`` is an enum.
@properties.usage: Usage policy assigned to the range.
@properties.description: Notes on the range's intended use.
@properties.requires_registration: True when tags in the range must be registered.
